title: CAPM: If an investor expects to earn 0.09 on a stock...
summary: Apply the Capital Asset Pricing Model to calculate expected returns or evaluate security pricing.
prompt: If an investor expects to earn 9% on a stock with a beta of 1.2, what is the expected return according to CAPM if the risk-free rate is 3%?
solution: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta × (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate) = 3% + 1.2(9% - 3%) = 3% + 7.2% = 10.2%.
